summary: Daring Other One jam, soaring Stella Blue, tight segues
blurb: The show delivers a monster 22‑minute The Other One excursion and an intense Stella Blue. China Cat Sunflower flows seamlessly into I Know You Rider, while the second set builds from blues numbers to soaring jams.
review: Seattle’s Paramount Northwest Theater on July 21, 1972 captured one of the Dead’s most adventurous nights of ’72. The first set rolls through classic material – a fiery Loser, crisp Mexicali Blues and a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition – before settling into a heartfelt Stella Blue that lets Jerry’s guitar sparkle. The second set is where the magic really erupts: Bill and Phil lock in on a thunder‑heavy Drums, then launch into an audacious 22‑minute stretch of The Other One where Phil even takes over the kit for a few verses, while Keith adds his tasteful keyboard textures. The night closes with a joyous burst of Sugar Magnolia and a sing‑along Not Fade Away, buoyed by Donna Jean’s guest vocals on Playing In The Band. Despite one SBD being a bit muffled, the performance shines through – daring interplay, soaring jams, and tight vocal harmonies make this a must‑listen slice of early‑’70s Dead history.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings describe the show in unequivocally positive terms – ‘daring jam’, ‘monster Around’, ‘stellar’, ‘intense Stella Blue’. Multiple reviewers praise the same highlights (The Other One, Stella Blue, China>Rider) and note exceptional interplay, giving a strong positive sentiment across sources.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard mix with clear instrument separation and minimal issues, showcasing the extended jams and vocal performances most vividly.

key_highlights: A monster 22‑minute The Other One jam with Phil taking over drums mid‑section.
Intense, emotionally resonant Stella Blue that lets Jerry’s tone shine.
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue delivering classic energy.
Guest vocals by Donna Jean on Playing In The Band adding extra sparkle.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone is praised throughout, especially on Jack Straw and Stella Blue; his vocals are noted as classic GD standards while he adds occasional tasteful fills.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh delivers daring interplay with Bill, even taking over drums during The Other One jam; his backing vocals lift Mexicali Blues, I Know You Rider, and Jack Straw.
band_performance.Bob: Weir provides colorful stage banter, introduces Donna Jean on Playing In The Band, and contributes solid vocal work that helps set the mellow vibe of the first set.
band_performance.Keith: Keyboard work is described as tasteful fills on Me & My Uncle and exploratory tinkering that adds texture without overpowering the band.
band_performance.Billy: Bill’s drumming blends well with Phil’s bass journey, anchoring the high‑energy second set even though the kit sits slightly back in the mix.
